jokes.text: I have actually packed on about 15 or 20 pounds of solid bulk muscle since that picture was taken, bro. It's actually a testament to your gym.
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to convince gym staff he's Dennis Reynolds using an old ID
jokes.punchline: He claims the ID doesn't match because he's gained so much muscle, turning his obvious lie into a compliment to the gym
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - delusional about his body while being caught in fraud, then spinning the lie into flattery. Great structure and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I have actually packed on about 15 or 20 pounds of solid bulk muscle since that picture was taken, bro. It's actually a testament to your gym.

jokes.text: Sweet camel toe. That's a good tuck job. You have it taped back there?
jokes.setup: Mac compliments Carmen's appearance after her transition
jokes.punchline: He compliments her genitalia in the most inappropriate, crude way possible
jokes.explanation: Peak Mac inappropriateness. Character-driven cringe but somewhat predictable for the show.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Sweet camel toe. That's a good tuck job. You have it taped back there?

jokes.text: Either way, now that you're dick-less, I'm into it.
jokes.setup: Mac expresses romantic interest in post-transition Carmen
jokes.punchline: He reduces his attraction to the absence of male genitalia in the crudest possible terms
jokes.explanation: Brutally direct and character-appropriate. The economy of 'dick-less' is perfectly Mac.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Either way, now that you're dick-less, I'm into it.

jokes.text: I found them in, like, in a little pile in the alleyway. A squirrel must have piled them up and then got hit by a car.
jokes.setup: Charlie offers almonds to everyone
jokes.punchline: Reveals he found them in an alley from a dead squirrel, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Charlie absurdity - the logic is completely insane but delivered with total confidence. Great escalation from normal to horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I found them in, like, in a little pile in the alleyway. A squirrel must have piled them up and then got hit by a car.

jokes.text: Why? Why? Why? Why? Why did you feed us alley almonds? Why wouldn't you tell us that?
jokes.setup: Learning about the source of the almonds
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horrified repeated 'Why?' questions
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Charlie's insanity. The repetition works for the disgust.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why? Why? Why? Why? Why did you feed us alley almonds? Why wouldn't you tell us that?

jokes.text: Mac's convoluted explanation about getting pumped up on protein from nuts then not getting his pump on but actually getting his pump on from pushups outside
jokes.setup: Mac trying to tell his gym story
jokes.punchline: Completely circular and nonsensical explanation that contradicts itself
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac rambling with perfect character voice - his need to sound impressive creates absurd logic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Mac's convoluted explanation about getting pumped up on protein from nuts then not getting his pump on but actually getting his pump on from pushups outside

jokes.text: She got her dick cut off, and she married a dude. Oh, that's great. Congratulations to her. No, that is not great. No? That is a gay marriage.
jokes.setup: Mac announces Carmen's marriage
jokes.punchline: His insistence that a transgender woman marrying a man is 'gay marriage'
jokes.explanation: Mac's warped logic and homophobia driving the plot. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She got her dick cut off, and she married a dude. Oh, that's great. Congratulations to her.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, that is not great.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No? That is a gay marriage.

jokes.text: Well, for starters, clearly, her husband is a homosexual, okay? Why are you making that assumption? Because he's with a tranny? You were with the tranny. What are you saying?
jokes.setup: Mac explains why Carmen's husband must be gay
jokes.punchline: Charlie points out Mac's hypocrisy since Mac also dated Carmen
jokes.explanation: Great logical trap that exposes Mac's inconsistency. Charlie as the voice of reason is always funny.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, for starters, clearly, her husband is a homosexual, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why are you making that assumption?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because he's with a tranny?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You were with the tranny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What are you saying?

jokes.text: No, Charlie, that is completely different, and you know it. Yeah. No. He's right. That's different, 'cause this new guy... if I'm hearing correctly... he waited until she got her dick cut off before he banged her.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to explain the difference between his situation and Carmen's husband's
jokes.punchline: Dennis supports Mac with equally flawed logic about timing
jokes.explanation: Absurd distinction that somehow makes sense to these characters. Dennis enabling Mac's delusion is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, Charlie, that is completely different, and you know it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. No. He's right. That's different, 'cause this new guy... if I'm hearing correctly... he waited until she got her dick cut off before he banged her.

jokes.text: Marriage is about procreation, okay? This is gay marriage. That's two dudes banging each other. What do you get from that? Nothing. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Mac explains his view on marriage
jokes.punchline: Reduces marriage to purely procreative function with crude language
jokes.explanation: Typical Mac homophobia and reductive thinking. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Marriage is about procreation, okay? This is gay marriage. That's two dudes banging each other. What do you get from that? Nothing. Nothing.

jokes.text: You should take your powerful extension cord argument straight to the Supreme Court. Mm-hmm. They'll love it there. Run that right up the ladder, right?
jokes.setup: Mac makes his marriage argument
jokes.punchline: Dennis sarcastically suggests Mac take his terrible logic to the Supreme Court
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic takedown. 'Extension cord argument' is a nice dismissive metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You should take your powerful extension cord argument straight to the Supreme Court. Mm-hmm. They'll love it there. Run that right up the ladder, right?

jokes.text: Where's our bible? Where's our goddamn bible? We don't have a bible in here. Yeah. Why don't we have a bible? We never have. It's a bar.
jokes.setup: Mac wants to cite the Bible to support his argument
jokes.punchline: Realizes they don't have a Bible in their bar, gets indignant about it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Mac's frustration. The obviousness of Dennis's response ('It's a bar') is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Where's our bible? Where's our goddamn bible? We don't have a bible in here. Yeah. Why don't we have a bible? We never have. It's a bar.

jokes.text: I don't have enough facts to support my argument. Clearly. But I'm gonna find a bible, and then I'm gonna come back, and I'm gonna school you sons of bitches
jokes.setup: Mac admits his argument lacks support
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares he'll prove them wrong anyway once he gets religious backup
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's stubborn refusal to change his mind despite lacking evidence.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't have enough facts to support my argument. Clearly. But I'm gonna find a bible, and then I'm gonna come back, and I'm gonna school you sons of bitches

jokes.text: I always thought I'd be married by now. What? What? Yeah. Well, I don't know. I... All that talk about marriage and everything... it's got me thinking about myself and my situation
jokes.setup: Dennis has an emotional revelation about marriage
jokes.punchline: His oversharing catches everyone off guard with its sincerity
jokes.explanation: Rare vulnerable Dennis moment that's uncomfortable for everyone. The others' shocked reactions sell it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I always thought I'd be married by now. What? What? Yeah. Well, I don't know. I... All that talk about marriage and everything... it's got me thinking about myself and my situation

jokes.text: I remember her dead tooth, dude. What? Dead tooth? She didn't have a dead tooth.
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions his high school girlfriend Maureen
jokes.punchline: Charlie remembers her having a dead tooth, which Dennis didn't notice
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's running gag. Charlie's random specific memory vs Dennis's romantic delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I remember her dead tooth, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What? Dead tooth? She didn't have a dead tooth.

jokes.text: You guys remember her brother, Bill? Bill Ponderosa? Boy, that guy was like a walking cliché. He was hot, great buns. Yeah, and I remember, every time you tried to talk to him, you'd (gags). I got a little gaggy.
jokes.setup: Dee mentions Bill Ponderosa
jokes.punchline: Reveals she would literally gag when trying to talk to him because he made her nervous
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific physical comedy. Sets up Dee's nervous condition around attractive people.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You guys remember her brother, Bill? Bill Ponderosa? Boy, that guy was like a walking cliché. He was hot, great buns.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I remember, every time you tried to talk to him, you'd (gags).

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I got a little gaggy.

jokes.text: Here, let me hit you with a two-by-four. You won't feel anything. No. I don't want to play that game anymore.
jokes.setup: Charlie is in pain from his back injury
jokes.punchline: Frank's solution is to cause more pain with a two-by-four to mask the original pain
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank logic - solve pain with more pain. Charlie's resigned 'not anymore' suggests this has happened before.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Here, let me hit you with a two-by-four. You won't feel anything.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No. I don't want to play that game anymore.

jokes.text: Frank and Charlie moving pennies with Charlie throwing them on the floor and Frank getting angry
jokes.setup: Frank trying to organize his penny collection
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete incompetence at simple task of moving pennies
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit that establishes Charlie's chaotic nature and Frank's impatience

dialogue.line: Frank and Charlie moving pennies with Charlie throwing them on the floor and Frank getting angry

jokes.text: If we do that domestic partnership thing that gay guys can do, I can totally get on your health insurance.
jokes.setup: Charlie needs medical care but can't afford it
jokes.punchline: His solution is to enter a gay marriage with Frank for health benefits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd solution that actually makes sense within Charlie's logic. Sets up the B-plot perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If we do that domestic partnership thing that gay guys can do, I can totally get on your health insurance.

jokes.text: Dee gagging while trying to express excitement about seeing Bill Ponderosa
jokes.setup: Dee is nervous about seeing Bill
jokes.punchline: She literally gags while trying to say his name
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows Dee's attraction/nervousness but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dee gagging while trying to express excitement about seeing Bill Ponderosa

jokes.text: Nobody really wants to see that. Goddamn, this has been a really fun walk down memory lane.
jokes.setup: Bill offers to show pictures of his kids
jokes.punchline: Dennis rudely declines then sarcastically calls the encounter 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis narcissism - can't even pretend to care about other people's children. The sarcasm is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Nobody really wants to see that. Goddamn, this has been a really fun walk down memory lane.

jokes.text: Dennis awkwardly trying to get Bill to leave by calling him a 'third wheel' who 'wormed his way into our situation'
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to be alone with Maureen
jokes.punchline: He rudely tries to dismiss Bill with transparent excuses
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic rudeness is well-executed cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're a third wheel who wormed his way into our situation

jokes.text: I had to break the window of the Datsun, so... Oh, yeah, it was one of those. One of those, huh? His eyes were so yellow.
jokes.setup: Details about the father's suicide
jokes.punchline: Maureen's matter-of-fact description of finding the body and the grotesque detail about yellow eyes
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny specific detail. The casual way she mentions the yellow eyes is perfectly disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: I had to break the window of the Datsun, so...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, it was one of those.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: One of those, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: His eyes were so yellow.

jokes.text: Maureen mentioning her father's 'yellow eyes' as an additional disturbing detail
jokes.setup: She's already revealed the suicide
jokes.punchline: She adds one more grotesque detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes an already dark moment even more disturbing

jokes.text: Men with men committing indecent acts... sex in the but, ... will receive the due penalty for their perversion. Word of the Lord.
jokes.setup: Mac reads from the Bible to support his argument
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'butt' as 'but' while trying to sound authoritative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's attempt at righteousness undermined by poor reading skills.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Men with men committing indecent acts... sex in the but, ... will receive the due penalty for their perversion. Word of the Lord.

jokes.text: When a man strikes his slave with a rod so hard that the slave dies, he shall be punished. If, however, the slave survives for a day or two, he is not to be punished, for the slave is his property.
jokes.setup: Mac quotes Bible verses about homosexuality
jokes.punchline: Nick counters with an equally problematic Bible verse about slavery
jokes.explanation: Excellent counter-argument that exposes Mac's selective Bible interpretation. The specific verse is darkly funny and effective.

dialogue.line: When a man strikes his slave with a rod so hard that the slave dies, he shall be punished. If, however, the slave survives for a day or two, he is not to be punished, for the slave is his property.

jokes.text: Well, so you're saying you're gonna whoop my black ass with a rod as long as you like it, as long as I get up after a day or two we are good? That's what you're telling me?
jokes.setup: Nick reads the slavery verse
jokes.punchline: He applies it directly to Mac's situation with him
jokes.explanation: Great personal application of the verse that makes Mac squirm. Direct and confrontational in the best way.

dialogue.speaker: Nick
dialogue.line: Well, so you're saying you're gonna whoop my black ass with a rod as long as you like it, as long as I get up after a day or two we are good? That's what you're telling me?

jokes.text: Nick reading the slavery passage about beating slaves with rods
jokes.setup: Mac is using the bible to condemn homosexuality
jokes.punchline: Nick finds an equally problematic passage about slavery
jokes.explanation: Excellent counter-argument that exposes the hypocrisy of selective biblical interpretation

dialogue.line: Nick reading the slavery passage about beating slaves with rods

jokes.text: Look, if anything, you're the one that had sex with me when I was a man. Oh, that's it... you're gay. No, no, no, no, no, no, no... He's gay.
jokes.setup: Mac calls Carmen gay for marrying a man
jokes.punchline: Carmen points out Mac had sex with her pre-transition, making Mac gay by his own logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical trap that completely destroys Mac's argument using his own flawed reasoning. Perfect escalation and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: Look, if anything, you're the one that had sex with me when I was a man.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, that's it... you're gay.

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no, no, no, no... He's gay.

jokes.text: Dennis and Dee are going to pull the plug on you so fast, your head is going to spin. But think about this, Frank, you know who's not going to do that? This guy right here.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains why Frank needs a domestic partner
jokes.punchline: Points out that Dennis and Dee would kill Frank for inheritance, but Charlie wouldn't
jokes.explanation: Great practical argument for their marriage. Charlie's twisted logic about family loyalty is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dennis and Dee are going to pull the plug on you so fast, your head is going to spin. But think about this, Frank, you know who's not going to do that? This guy right here.

jokes.text: You just had to sign a little piece of paper. They gave Frank and I, like, a million forms for our marriage.
jokes.setup: Dennis got married easily
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals the bureaucratic nightmare of gay marriage paperwork
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary disguised as character complaint. The contrast in paperwork is well-observed.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You just had to sign a little piece of paper. They gave Frank and I, like, a million forms for our marriage.

jokes.text: Did you smell her breath? Are you kidding me? It smelled like she was nibbling on little pieces of shit.
jokes.setup: Dennis just married his high school sweetheart
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains about her breath in the most disgusting way possible
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism - can't even enjoy his wedding without finding fault. The grossness is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Did you smell her breath? Are you kidding me? It smelled like she was nibbling on little pieces of shit.

jokes.text: I built up a shell. A shell around myself. A cold, calculated, heartless shell that couldn't be broken by anything but marriage. And that's what she did, dude... she broke that shell with marriage.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to explain his feelings
jokes.punchline: His overwrought metaphor about having a 'shell' broken by marriage
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pseudo-psychology and overblown metaphors are perfectly in character, though a bit verbose.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I built up a shell. A shell around myself. A cold, calculated, heartless shell that couldn't be broken by anything but marriage. And that's what she did, dude... she broke that shell with marriage.

jokes.text: You know what, believe it or not, I've been doing some inventory... and it turns out you don't actually own anything in this apartment. That can't be true. It's shockingly true.
jokes.setup: Dennis kicks Mac out of their apartment
jokes.punchline: Reveals Mac owns literally nothing in the apartment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis power move. The casual cruelty and Mac's shocked reaction work beautifully together.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You know what, believe it or not, I've been doing some inventory... and it turns out you don't actually own anything in this apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That can't be true.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's shockingly true.

jokes.text: For instance, that shirt that you're wearing right now. That was my shirt. And then I got tired of it, I threw it in the trash and then you fished it out of the trash and you cut the sleeves off it, as you are want to do, 'cause that's your thing, but whatever.
jokes.setup: Dennis claims Mac owns nothing
jokes.punchline: Reveals Mac's shirt is Dennis's discarded trash that Mac modified
jokes.explanation: Devastating character work. Shows the complete power imbalance and Mac's pathetic dependence. The sleeve detail is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: For instance, that shirt that you're wearing right now. That was my shirt. And then I got tired of it, I threw it in the trash and then you fished it out of the trash and you cut the sleeves off it, as you are want to do, 'cause that's your thing, but whatever.

jokes.text: Neither of you would be the woman. You're both men. Are you kidding me? Is this what you've been upset about the whole time? Which one of us is going to be the woman?
jokes.setup: Frank asks who would be the woman in a gay marriage
jokes.punchline: Carmen's exasperation at their fundamental misunderstanding of how gay relationships work
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the resolution. Their confusion about gay relationships being heteronormative is well-played.

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: Neither of you would be the woman. You're both men. Are you kidding me? Is this what you've been upset about the whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Which one of us is going to be the woman?

jokes.text: I'm not going to get my dick cut off and sold to China. Spend the rest of my life on a treadmill like this guy.
jokes.setup: Frank's fear about being the 'woman' in the relationship
jokes.punchline: His bizarre assumption about what happens to removed genitalia and stereotypes about women exercising
jokes.explanation: Absolutely insane Frank logic. 'Sold to China' is completely random and perfect. The treadmill stereotype adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm not going to get my dick cut off and sold to China. Spend the rest of my life on a treadmill like this guy.

jokes.text: Two dudes getting married... that doesn't seem very gay. Yeah, right? It's not... Yeah, right, yeah, okay. Two cool married... dudes. Not gay.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains they'd both still be men
jokes.punchline: They convince themselves that two men getting married isn't gay because they're both masculine
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular logic that resolves their concerns through complete denial of reality. The repetition sells their self-deception.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Two dudes getting married... that doesn't seem very gay.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, right?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's not...

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, right, yeah, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Two cool married... dudes. Not gay.

jokes.text: Hey, did they really sell your dick to China? No. I can tell by your stare the answer is no.
jokes.setup: Frank's earlier comment about dicks being sold to China
jokes.punchline: Frank asks Carmen directly, then immediately realizes the absurdity from her reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Frank's genuine confusion about his own absurd statement. Carmen's silent reaction is the punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Hey, did they really sell your dick to China? No. I can tell by your stare the answer is no.

jokes.text: Oh. What are you doing? Are you going to throw up? No... Then why are you gagging? Oh, that... Because, Dee, that triggers my gag reflex too, and then I feel like I'm going to throw up.
jokes.setup: Dee starts gagging
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals he also has a gag reflex triggered by Dee's gagging, creating a feedback loop
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the gagging theme. The idea of sympathetic gagging creating a loop is nicely absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh. What are you doing? Are you going to throw up?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: No...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Then why are you gagging?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, that...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because, Dee, that triggers my gag reflex too, and then I feel like I'm going to throw up.

jokes.text: Bill Ponderosa.
jokes.setup: Mac sees the heavily changed Bill Ponderosa
jokes.punchline: His shocked recognition after not recognizing him at all
jokes.explanation: Simple recognition beat. The economy is good but the moment is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Bill Ponderosa.

jokes.text: Although it gets a little confusing halfway through because Mac and I decided to switch parts. He wanted to play Murtaugh for some reason. Yeah, and I'm not going to lie. His use of blackface I found a bit regrettable, but...
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions their homemade Lethal Weapon movie
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals Mac used blackface to play the Black character
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold. The casual way Dennis mentions something horrific while calling it 'regrettable' is perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Although it gets a little confusing halfway through because Mac and I decided to switch parts. He wanted to play Murtaugh for some reason. Yeah, and I'm not going to lie. His use of blackface I found a bit regrettable, but...

jokes.text: Dennis describing his homemade Lethal Weapon 5 movie where Mac used blackface
jokes.setup: Dennis is showing Maureen his movie collection
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions problematic creative choices
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that reveals their amateur filmmaking and poor judgment

dialogue.line: Dennis describing his homemade Lethal Weapon 5 movie where Mac used blackface

jokes.text: I see that you're wearing another one of those kitty cat sweatshirts that you seem so fond of. And you glued some jewels and some flowers and things to it. Uh-huh. Interesting.
jokes.setup: Dennis notices Maureen's fashion choices
jokes.punchline: His barely concealed disdain for her cat-themed, bedazzled clothing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dennis's buyer's remorse. His attempt to be polite while being horrified works well.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I see that you're wearing another one of those kitty cat sweatshirts that you seem so fond of. And you glued some jewels and some flowers and things to it. Uh-huh. Interesting.

jokes.text: Uh, I love you.
jokes.setup: Maureen says 'I love you' to Dennis
jokes.punchline: Dennis's hesitant, uncomfortable response after a long pause
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat. The pause before his reluctant response shows his complete regret about the marriage. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Maureen
dialogue.line: Uh, I love you.
